actions.intent: Begin by shaping the lower puppy's route instead of drifting into upper-board value.
Take a safe stabilizer while keeping the lower rescue as the priority.
Open the lower puppy's water-facing side with a clean route clear.
Continue the lower route and keep dock residue manageable.
Finish the lower route setup before pivoting upward.
Use a dock-safe clear that preserves the lower-first rescue margin.
Pivot after the urgent puppy is secured enough to finish cleanly.
Open the safer upper puppy route without broad side clearing.
Convert the remaining lower-side material into the planned rescue sequence.
Finish the upper puppy after the lower-first lesson has landed.

notes: Designer golden for the release beat: lower puppy first with generous water margin, then a calm upper rescue.
